iteration_basis: iter01 visual gap inspection: stronger avatar silhouette, denser skyline, segmented curved desk lip, visible monitor arm spines and hinges
reference_targets: six wide rectangular monitor surfaces plus upper articulated monitor family
raised central console with dense status controls
sloped touch tablets on left and right desk wings
panoramic rainy neon skyline window shell
central cyan/magenta holographic female presence silhouette
visual_improvements_over_iter01: Segmented neon lip makes the front desk read more curved/wrapped instead of one flat slab
Avatar gains hair lobes, neck, shoulders, lower body, legs, and scan-sheet accents
Window gains more micro city columns and horizontal neon horizon bands
Monitor arms gain visible vertical spines and hinge cylinders
Object count increased to 259 with deterministic repo/spike GLB hash match
known_gaps_next_iteration: Avatar remains too abstract/glowing; next pass needs a clearer anime/female mesh silhouette with face/hair/torso separation and lower opacity so shape is visible
Desk is still built from primitives; next pass should use bevelled curved topology rather than segmented rectangular approximations
Monitor faces still lack real UI texture maps, bezels, clamps, cabling, and rear mechanical detail
Skyline remains decorative bars; next pass needs layered billboards/rain planes/depth and stronger outside-city parallax
Scene still needs runtime placement/load validation inside the active Three.js/WebGL ARDA layer
